---
title: "Seminar Presentation"
author: "Julie Wilson"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Load Packages

```{r load_packages}

library("pacman")
library("tidyverse")
library("maps")
library(dplyr)
library(did)
library(bacondecomp)
library(fixest)
library(modelsummary)
library(infer)
library(ggplot2)
library(car)
library(plm)
library(readr)
library(broom)
```

## Import Data

```{r data_import}

df_all <- read.csv("C:\\Users\\julie\\Downloads\\Seminar research\\more r studio\\Final_seminar\\all_data.csv")


glimpse(df_all)
```

### Checking for missing data

```{r missing_data_check}


sum(is.na(df_all))

colSums(is.na(df_all))

df_missing_data <- colSums(is.na(df_all))

which(is.na(df_all), arr.ind = TRUE)

df_all <-  df_prelim[-1651, ]

sum(is.na(df_all))
```

### creating policy groups

```{r creating_policy_groups}
# Recreate df_all cleanly (removes the broken policy_group from the bad merge)
df_all <- df_all[, !names(df_all) %in% "policy_group"]

# Assign policy group directly using the lookup
state_min <- tapply(df_all$gestational_limit_weeks, df_all$State, min)

df_all$policy_group <- ifelse(state_min[df_all$State] == 0, "Total Ban",
                       ifelse(state_min[df_all$State] <= 6, "Severely Restrictive",
                       ifelse(state_min[df_all$State] <= 12, "Moderately Restrictive",
                       ifelse(state_min[df_all$State] <= 18, "Moderately Permissive",
                       "Permissive"))))

# Check
table(df_all$policy_group)
nrow(df_all)  # should match your original row count

# Get unique state-to-policy_group mapping (no duplicates)
state_policy_map <- unique(df_all[, c("State", "policy_group")])

# Sort and print
state_policy_map <- state_policy_map[order(state_policy_map$policy_group, state_policy_map$State), ]
print(state_policy_map)

# Count of states per group (not rows)
table(state_policy_map$policy_group)

# See states listed by group
split(state_policy_map$State, state_policy_map$policy_group)
```
